```{=html}
<aside class="side-panel scorecard-sidebar">
<details open>
<summary>Reading the validation plots</summary>
<dl>
  <dt><code>z = (MC − cf) / stderr</code></dt>
  <dd><code>|z| ≤ 2</code> is sampling noise; <code>|z| &gt; 3</code>
  points at a simulator bug.</dd>

  <dt><code>±CI<sub>95</sub> = 1.96 · SD / √n</code></dt>
  <dd>95% half-width of the MC mean. Shrinks as
  <code>1 / √n<sub>paths</sub></code>.</dd>

  <dt>Variance ratio</dt>
  <dd>Fee and back-to-back satisfy
  <code>Var[Π<sub>b2b</sub>] / Var[R<sub>fee</sub>] = (P / f)²</code>
  — algebraic, not statistical. Any MC disagreement is a bug.</dd>

  <dt>Partial / syndicated SD scaling</dt>
  <dd><code>SD[Π<sub>α,β</sub>] = (1 − α)(1 − β) · SD[Π<sub>b2b</sub>]</code>.
  The expected signature of every sweep plot on the page.</dd>

  <dt>VaR / CVaR on the fee book</dt>
  <dd>Read as "low end of revenue", not loss — fee revenue is
  <code>≥ 0</code> by construction.</dd>

  <dt>Merton row</dt>
  <dd>Expect <code>|z| ≲ 2</code> against the GBM closed form
  (compensation is firing) and SD (merton) strictly above SD (gbm).
  Flat SD means the overlay is inert.</dd>

  <dt>Switching row</dt>
  <dd>Closed-form columns are <code>NaN</code> — no closed-form
  density. Compare MC tails directly to the no-switch anchor.</dd>
</dl>
</details>
</aside>
```
